title: The Temporal Sequence of Party Leader Incongruence
description: This dataset comprises high-density EEG recordings from 44 participants engaged in two complementary tasks designed to investigate the temporal dynamics of neural responses to political incongruence. Using a preregistered design with supporters of progressive and far-right Dutch political parties, the study disentangles identity-based incongruence (automatic responses to party leaders) from source-content incongruence (processing of incongruent political messages). Behavioral ratings and event annotations accompany the neuroimaging data, enabling investigation of the 'Hot Cognition' hypothesis regarding partisan information processing.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a 64-channel BioSemi ActiveTwo system with electrodes positioned according to the International 10-20 layout, including EOG, ECG, and mastoid leads. Two experimental tasks were employed: (1) Just Faces Task with 260 trials of 700ms face presentations to measure automatic neural responses to political identity, and (2) Statements & Faces Task with 240 trials using a 2×2 factorial design (in-party/out-party × pro-attitudinal/counter-attitudinal statements) with statement durations of 1830–2880ms followed by face presentations of 1200–1400ms. Data were recorded with CMS/DRL reference and offline band-pass filtered (0.5–40 Hz). Behavioral measures included Likert-scale ratings of agreement, surprise, and upset, plus reaction times.

funding_references.award_title: IP-PAD: Horizon Europe MSCA Doctoral Networks
